title: centaurea micranthos var. recurvata
intro: Centaurea micranthos var. recurvata, also known as Albanian cornflower, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, inflammation, and as an anti-microbial agent. The plant is said to possess health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Centaurea micranthos var. recurvata can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and is traditionally used in herbal remedies to address various health issues.
